title: D&D 5e 2024 Mounts and Vehicles
description: Complete list of mounts, vehicles, and transportation options

mounts.name: Camel
mounts.description: Hardy desert mount capable of long journeys without water

mounts.name: Elephant
mounts.description: Massive mount with exceptional carrying capacity

mounts.name: Horse, Draft
mounts.description: Strong working horse bred for pulling heavy loads

mounts.name: Horse, Riding
mounts.description: Standard riding horse for travel and transportation

mounts.name: Mastiff
mounts.description: Large dog suitable for smaller riders or as a pack animal

mounts.name: Mule
mounts.description: Sturdy and reliable pack animal, excellent for rough terrain

mounts.name: Pony
mounts.description: Small horse suitable for smaller riders

mounts.name: Warhorse
mounts.description: Trained for combat, brave and loyal in battle

tackAndHarness.name: Carriage
tackAndHarness.description: Enclosed passenger vehicle for comfortable travel

tackAndHarness.name: Cart
tackAndHarness.description: Simple two-wheeled vehicle for transporting goods

tackAndHarness.name: Chariot
tackAndHarness.description: Fast two-wheeled vehicle for racing or combat

tackAndHarness.name: Feed per day
tackAndHarness.description: Daily food requirement for one mount

tackAndHarness.name: Saddle, Exotic
tackAndHarness.description: Required for riding aquatic or flying mounts

tackAndHarness.name: Saddle, Military
tackAndHarness.description: Gives Advantage on ability checks to remain mounted

tackAndHarness.name: Saddle, Riding
tackAndHarness.description: Standard saddle with bit, bridle, reins, and other equipment

tackAndHarness.name: Sled
tackAndHarness.description: Winter vehicle for snow and ice travel

tackAndHarness.name: Stabling per day
tackAndHarness.description: Daily cost for housing and caring for a mount

tackAndHarness.name: Wagon
tackAndHarness.description: Four-wheeled vehicle for transporting heavy loads

largeVehicles.name: Airship
largeVehicles.description: Magical flying vessel for air travel

largeVehicles.name: Galley
largeVehicles.description: Large warship with both oars and sails

largeVehicles.name: Keelboat
largeVehicles.description: Small sailing vessel for rivers and lakes

largeVehicles.name: Longship
largeVehicles.description: Viking-style vessel with oars and sail

largeVehicles.name: Rowboat
largeVehicles.description: Small boat that can be carried and rowed

largeVehicles.name: Warship
largeVehicles.description: Military vessel equipped for naval combat

bardingRules.description: Barding is armor designed for a mount. Any type of armor can be purchased as barding.
bardingRules.note: The cost is four times the normal cost, and it weighs twice as much.

cargoRules.description: An animal pulling a vehicle can move weight up to five times its base carrying capacity, including the weight of the vehicle.
cargoRules.multipleAnimals: If multiple animals pull the same vehicle, add their carrying capacities together.

windConditions.strongWind: A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed
windConditions.deadCalm: In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can't move under sail and must be rowed

currentEffects.downstream: Add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle
currentEffects.upstream: These vehicles can't be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores

repair.cityModifier: Repair time and cost are halved in a city shipyard
